raw_consumer_statement:
I reserved a rental car through XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX XXXX from XXXX - XXXX I paid cash to rent the car, but gave my Citibank card for a security deposit. In which they charged my card {$1000.00} On XXXX I received a confirmation text message from XXXX, stating that the contract was successfully closed and my deposit would be refunded within 7-15 business days. It never was. 
I started a dispute of the charge with Citibank on XXXX On XXXX I received an email from Citi saying the dispute has been closed and the charge plus any incurred interest would be reversed back to me. I called Citi and asked to be sent all correspondence they received from XXXX What they sent was a copy of my ID, credit card, pictures of the car, and a charge information sheet. Nothing showing the {$1000.00} deposit charge or my signature. 
I called the dispute department at Citi to state that there was nothing sent addressing the {$1000.00} deposit charged to my card so how can they accept what XXXX sent as any kind of proof? I even emailed the CEO of Citi, XXXX XXXX, and was contacted by someone in executive escalations. They listened to my case and said they would look into it again, but couldnt promise anything as I willingly used my card. I said even though they gave no mention, proof or reason to why they havent returned my deposit and I sent proof of a message stating it would be returned. I also informed her that XXXX seems to be a fraudulent business since the phone numbers are no longer in service and the XXXX said even corporate doesnt respond to them.
